name: First-Citizens Bank & Trust Company
description_short: First Citizens Bank & Trust Company is a full-service bank offering personal checking, savings, credit cards, mortgages, loans, and wealth management services for individuals and businesses.
description_long:
First Citizens Bank & Trust Company operates as a comprehensive financial institution providing banking services across personal, small business, commercial, and wealth segments. The bank traces its operations through a substantial online presence at firstcitizens.com, offering digital-first banking solutions alongside traditional branch services.

The company's service portfolio spans personal banking (checking accounts at multiple tiers including Primary, Premier, and Prestige Checking), savings products (CDs, Money Market, Online Savings), credit cards with various reward structures (Cash Rewards, Travel Rewards, Secured Cash Back), mortgage products (fixed-rate, adjustable-rate, VA/FHA/USDA loans, jumbo loans, refinancing), consumer loans (auto, home equity, home improvement), and retirement accounts (Traditional IRA, Roth IRA, SEP IRA, SIMPLE IRA). The bank also offers investment services, insurance products, and educational resources across banking, investing, retirement, and financial planning topics.

First Citizens distinguishes itself through specialized lending expertise across multiple sectors including agribusiness, healthcare, manufacturing, nonprofits, and skilled trades. For commercial clients, the bank provides asset-based lending, capital markets services, commercial real estate financing, merchant services, and treasury management. The wealth division offers trust services, private banking, institutional asset management, retirement plan services, and charitable advising—services typically associated with larger regional or national banks.

As a traditional bank with broad product offerings, First Citizens serves as a general-purpose financial institution. However, prospective customers should note that the website provides limited information about specific terms, rates, fees, or minimum balance requirements. The breadth of services suggests this bank competes on convenience and relationship banking rather than specialized lending or alternative financial products.
services: Primary, Premier, and Prestige Checking Accounts
Savings accounts including Certificates of Deposit and Money Market accounts
Credit cards with cash rewards, travel rewards, and secured cash back options
Fixed-rate and adjustable-rate mortgages, jumbo loans, VA/FHA/USDA loans, home construction loans, mortgage refinancing
Auto loans, home equity loans, and home improvement loans
Traditional IRA, Roth IRA, SEP IRA, and SIMPLE IRA retirement accounts
Business checking, payables, receivables, and liquidity management services
Business loans, SBA term loans, USDA business loans, and equipment financing
Commercial real estate financing and asset-based lending
Trust and fiduciary services including Delaware Trust Services and Donor Advised Funds
pros: Comprehensive service offerings including checking, savings, credit cards, mortgages, auto loans, and investment accounts under one institution
Multiple checking account tiers (Primary, Premier, Prestige) allowing customers to select accounts matching their banking needs and activity levels
Specialized lending programs for specific professions and industries (Medical Professional Mortgages, Agribusiness lending, Cannabis Banking, Nonprofit services)
Extensive mortgage product range including VA, FHA, USDA loans, jumbo mortgages, home construction loans, and renovation financing
Wealth management and trust services including Delaware Trust Services, Donor Advised Funds, and institutional asset management for high-net-worth clients
Digital banking capabilities including online savings accounts, self-directed and guided investing options, and digital-first banking tools
Robust educational resources including calculators, financial guides, insights on banking/investing/retirement, and 2026 financial planning guides
cons: Website does not disclose specific APRs, fees, minimum balance requirements, or promotional rates for any products, requiring customers to contact the bank or apply for rate quotes
No information provided about FDIC insurance limits, account protection details, or regulatory oversight specifics on the public-facing website
Limited transparency regarding business loan terms, SBA loan requirements, equipment financing rates, or commercial credit card benefits and conditions
Emphasis on multiple service lines (personal, business, commercial, wealth) may result in less specialized expertise or competitive pricing versus niche-focused competitors
Website navigation is complex with numerous nested menu categories, potentially making it difficult for customers to locate specific product details or compare offerings
best_for: Customers seeking a one-stop financial institution for checking, savings, credit cards, mortgages, and investment services
Business owners and entrepreneurs in specialized industries (healthcare, agribusiness, nonprofits) looking for industry-specific lending expertise
High-net-worth individuals and families requiring wealth management, trust services, private banking, and institutional investment solutions
Homebuyers seeking specialized mortgage products including VA/FHA/USDA loans, medical professional mortgages, or jumbo financing
diagnosis: First Citizens Bank & Trust Company is best for customers seeking comprehensive banking services—checking, savings, lending, mortgages, and investments—from a single traditional bank, particularly those in specialized industries or high-net-worth individuals needing wealth management. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal information on rates, fees, and terms; customers must contact the bank directly or submit applications to obtain specific pricing and product details.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
